title: Techniques to support heterogeneous network data path discovery
abstract: Methods, systems, and devices are described for discovering and establishing an optimal data path between a source device and a destination device of a first network by employing one or more other networks having a network protocol different from the first network. The techniques of the present disclosure may include receiving a route request message from the source device at an edge device connected to both a first network and a second network, and forwarding the route request message via the second network. In some examples, the data path that offers an improved link metric and/or a minimum hop count may be established by routing traffic along a data path employing a plurality of networks (e.g., WLAN, cellular or Ethernet) between the source device and the destination device.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of communication networking, comprising: receiving, at an edge device connected to a first network and a second network, a route request message from a source device via the first network; identifying, at the edge device, a destination device within the first network based at least in part on the route request message; forwarding the route request message via the second network; and determining, based at least in part on the forwarded route request message, whether traffic between the source device and the destination device that is routed through the edge device should be directed via the first network or via the second network. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the determining comprises: receiving a route reply message from the destination device, the route reply message based at least in part on the forwarded route request message. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the determining comprises: determining, based at least in part on the forwarded route request message, that a data path via the second network provides at least one of a favorable link metric, or minimum hop count, or a combination thereof between the source device and the destination device as compared to a data path via the first network. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein forwarding the route request message via the second network comprises: incrementing a hop count of the route request message. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein forwarding the route request message via the second network comprises: tunneling a packet comprising the route request message and having a format associated with a protocol employed by the first network inside a frame having a format associated with a protocol employed by the second network; and broadcasting the frame over the second network. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the frame comprises an identification that the source device and the destination device are within the first network. 7. The method of claim 5, wherein the frame comprises at least one of a neighbor awareness network (NAN) cluster identification (ID), a NAN data link group ID, a mesh ID, a medium access control (MAC) address of the source device, or a MAC address of the destination device. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein determining whether the traffic should be directed via the first network or via the second network is based at least in part on one or both of: a predetermined characteristic of a data path from the edge device to the destination device via the first network, or a predetermined characteristic of a data path from the edge device to the destination device via the second network. 9. The method of claim 1, further comprising: receiving, at the edge device, a data packet originating from the source device to be received by the destination device; encapsulating the data packet inside a data frame having a format associated with the second network; and forwarding the data frame from the edge device along an established data path via the second network, the established data path based at least in part on the determining whether traffic should be directed via the first network or via the second network. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the second network employs a network protocol different from a network protocol employed by the first network. 11. The method of claim 1 , wherein the route request message is a path request message (PREQ) according to a hybrid wireless mesh protocol (HWMP). 12. A method for communication networking, comprising: receiving, at an edge device connected to a first network and a second network, a frame via the second network; extracting, from the frame, a packet comprising a route request message from a source device of the first network to a destination device of the first network; and 

 forwarding, via the first network, the packet comprising the route request message via the first network. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising: determining the destination device, prior to extracting the packet from the frame, based at least in part on the frame. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the frame comprises at least one of a neighbor awareness network (NAN) cluster identification (ID), a NAN data link group ID, a mesh ID, a medium access control (MAC) address of the source device, or a MAC address of the destination device. 15. The method of claim 12, further comprising: receiving, at the edge device, a route reply message from the destination device via the first network, the route reply message based at least in part on the forwarded packet comprising the route request message; and forwarding the route reply message via the second network. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein forwarding the route reply message comprises: tunneling a reply packet comprising the route reply message and having a format associated with a protocol employed by the first network inside a reply frame having a format associated with a protocol employed by the second network. 17. The method of claim 12, further comprising: receiving a data frame at the edge device; determining the destination device based at least in part on the data frame; extracting a data packet from the data frame; and forwarding the data packet along an established data path from the edge device via the first network, the established data path based at least in part on the forwarded packet comprising the route request message. 18. The method of claim 12, wherein the second network employs a network protocol different from a network protocol employed by the first network. 19. The method of claim 12, wherein the route request message is a path request message (PREQ) according to a hybrid wireless mesh protocol (HWMP). 20. An apparatus for communication networking, comprising: a first receiver for receiving, at an edge device connected to a first network and a second network, a route request message from a source device over the first network; a destination identifier for identifying, at the edge device, a destination device within the first network based at least in part on the route request message; a transmitter for forwarding the route request message via the second network; and a heterogeneous network communication manager for determining, based at least in part on the forwarded route request message, whether traffic between the source device and the destination device that is routed through the edge device should be directed via the first network or via the second network. 21. The apparatus of claim 20, wherein the heterogeneous network communication manager is operable to: tunnel a packet comprising the route request message and having a format associated with a protocol employed by the first network inside a frame having a format associated with a protocol employed by the second network. 22. The apparatus of claim 21, wherein the transmitter is operable to: broadcast the frame over the second network. 23. The apparatus of claim 20, further comprising: a second receiver for receiving, via the second network, a route reply message from the destination device, the route reply message based at least in part on the forwarded route request message. 24. The apparatus of claim 23, wherein the heterogeneous network communication manager is operable to determine whether traffic should be directed via the first network or via the second network based at least in part on the received route reply message. 25. The apparatus of claim 20, wherein: the first receiver is operable to receive, via the first network, a data packet originating from the source device to be received by the destination device; the heterogeneous network communication manager is operable to encapsulate the data packet inside a data frame having a format associated with the second network; and the transmitter is operable to forward, the data frame along an established data path via the second network, the established data path based at least in part on the determination of the heterogeneous network communication manager of whether traffic should be directed via the first network or via the second network. 26. The apparatus of claim 20, wherein the second network employs a network protocol different from a network protocol employed by the first network. 27. An apparatus for communication networking, comprising: a first receiver to receive, at an edge device connected to a first network and a second network, a frame via the second network; a heterogeneous network communication manager to extract, from the frame, a packet comprising a route request message from a source device of the first network to a destination device of the first network; and a first transmitter for forwarding, via the first network, the packet comprising the route request message via the first network. 28. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the heterogeneous network communication manager is operable to determine the destination device, prior to extracting the packet from the frame, based at least in part on the frame. 29. The apparatus of claim 27, further comprising: a second receiver to receive, at the edge device, a route reply message from the destination device via the first network, the route reply message based at least in part on the forwarded packet comprising the route request message; and a second transmitter to forward the route reply message via the second network. 30. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein: 

 the first receiver is operable to receive a data frame via the second network; the heterogeneous network communication manager is operable to determine the destination device based at least in part on the data frame, and extract a data packet from the data frame; and the first transmitter is operable to forward the data packet along an established data path via the first network, the established data path based at least in part on the forwarded packet comprising the route request message.
